mnemonics.title: TahoR-NR-BiC-BBR
mnemonics.content: TahoR-NeW-ReNo-BiC-BBR - Think 'Tale of Two Roosters Needing Ribs, BIBs, and BBQ' to remember: Tahoe, Reno, NewReno, BIC, BBR

mnemonics.title: Slow Start = SS
mnemonics.content: SS starts Small: cwnd = 1 MSS, then doubles every RTT (exponential growth) - Remember: 'Starting Small, Growing Steadily'

mnemonics.title: AIMD Rule
mnemonics.content: Additive Increase Multiplicative Decrease: Add 1 MSS per RTT in congestion avoidance, Multiply cwnd by 0.5 on loss - Remember: 'Add AIP (1) to Multiply, Avoid Pain'

mnemonics.title: Fast Retransmit 3 Dups
mnemonics.content: On 3 duplicate ACKs, retransmit without waiting for timeout - Remember: '3 Dups? Dump (retransmit) Fast!'

mnemonics.title: CUBIC Formula
mnemonics.content: W(t) = C(t - K)³ + Wmax where K = ∛(Wmax × β / C) - Remember: 'CUBIC uses cubic function, C is constant, K is root time to reach Wmax'

mnemonics.title: BBR Beats Bufferbloat
mnemonics.content: BBR = Bottleneck Bandwidth and RTT; focuses on actual path capacity, not loss - Remember: 'BBR Busts Bufferbloat By Recognizing bandwidth and round-trip delay'

mnemonics.title: Vegas Detects Early
mnemonics.content: TCP Vegas measures actual RTT vs expected, detects congestion before loss - Remember: 'Vegas Visions Early Via RTT Variation'

mnemonics.title: SACK Selects
mnemonics.content: Selective Acknowledgment allows non-contiguous block recovery - Remember: 'SACK Says Acknowledge Specifically, Collect All Chunks Kept'

acronyms.usage: The maximum amount of data that can be sent before receiving an ACK. Grows in slow start, slows in congestion avoidance
Threshold determining whether to use slow start (cwnd < sssthresh) or congestion avoidance (cwnd >= sssthresh)
Time for packet to travel to destination and back; used to calculate RTO and measure network delay
Largest TCP payload size; typically 1460 bytes in Ethernet networks
Core congestion control algorithm: increase window linearly, decrease exponentially on loss
Timer value for retransmitting unacknowledged segments; based on smoothed RTT and variance
Congestion control algorithm by Google; models pipe capacity using bandwidth and RTT measurements
TCP option allowing receiver to specify non-contiguous data blocks received correctly
Extension to SACK; tracks highest forward ACK to better estimate data in flight during recovery
Uses binary search to find the new cwnd after loss; predecessor to CUBIC
Default Linux congestion control; uses cubic function for window growth, Wmax for recovery
Mechanism to signal congestion without packet loss; routers mark packets, receivers echo back

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a ball doubling in size: 1→2→4→8→16... Each RTT the ball doubles until it hits the threshold
Picture a staircase: steps going up (additive increase) then falling off a cliff (multiplicative decrease) - this is the classic sawtooth pattern
Think of a 'W' shape: starts flat (slow start), rises steeply (max growth), then levels near Wmax (congestion avoidance), drops on loss, then grows from new point
BBR is like a smart driver who checks speedometer (bandwidth) and clock (RTT); other variants are like drivers who only notice when they crash (packet loss)
Imagine a pipeline with leaks (losses); fast recovery is like patching leaks quickly while keeping flow going, rather than draining and restarting the entire pipeline
Picture a thermostat (Vegas) detecting heat (congestion) before the room overheats (packet loss), while other variants wait for the fire (loss) to react
Think of a mosaic: instead of saying 'I got pieces 1-5 and 7-10' (cumulative ACK), SACK says 'I got pieces 1-5 AND 7-10' - showing exactly what's missing

analogies.analogy: Like a cautious driver: starts slow (1 car), speeds up (doubles), sees red light (loss), stops completely (timeout), then restarts from minimum speed
Like an experienced driver: when encountering small issues (duplicate ACKs), slows down moderately and continues; only stops completely for major issues (timeout)
Like Reno but remembers all partially acknowledged data during recovery - like a driver who tracks exactly which passengers (packets) are still waiting at each stop
Like a competitive cyclist: starts slow, accelerates aggressively to catch up to the pack (Wmax), then maintains a steady pace near the leader once caught
Like a smart delivery driver: uses GPS (bandwidth+RTT) to find the optimal speed that fills the truck without hitting traffic, rather than speeding up until crashes
Like a car with early warning system: detects traffic buildup (increased RTT) before accidents happen (packet loss), and slows down proactively
Like a thermostat: turns heat up slowly (additive) when cold, but turns it off completely (multiplicative) when too hot - conservative on danger, aggressive on safety
Like a newborn baby learning to walk: starts with tiny steps, rapidly gains confidence, until hitting an obstacle then starts over
Like a garden hose: cwnd is the water pressure; too much pressure (large cwnd) causes bursts (loss), too little wastes capacity
Like a highway with no exit ramp: cars (packets) pile up in the buffer, causing huge delays, even when traffic (bandwidth) has cleared - BBR solves this by not filling the buffer
